sheets.title: Associate in Applied Science
sheets.overview: The Associate Degree Nursing curriculum prepares individuals with the knowledge and skills necessary to provide nursing care to clients across the lifespan. The curriculum equips learners with competencies to integrate safety and quality into nursing care across dynamic settings, employing evidence-based practice, quality improvement, and informatics while developing critical thinking skills as an interdisciplinary team member.
sheets.employmentOutlook: Graduates are eligible to apply to take the National Council Licensure Examination (NCLEX-RN) and pursue employment in acute, chronic, extended, industrial, and community healthcare settings. The program holds full approval from the North Carolina Board of Nursing and accreditation from the Accreditation Commission for Education in Nursing (ACEN).

rows.name: Quantitative Literacy or Precalculus Algebra (MAT 143 or MAT 171)

sheets.title: Diploma
sheets.overview: The Practical Nursing curriculum is a one-year (three semesters) diploma program that provides knowledge, skills, and strategies to integrate safety and quality into nursing care, and practice in a dynamic environment. The curriculum emphasizes the practical nurse role as a dependent role under the supervision of the registered nurse (RN), as a member of the interdisciplinary team, providing safe, individualized care while employing evidence-based practice, quality improvement, and informatics.
sheets.employmentOutlook: Graduates may apply for the National Council Licensure Examination (NCLEX-PN) and pursue employment within acute, chronic, extended, industrial, and community healthcare facilities.

sheets.title: Certificate
sheets.subtitle: Nurse Aide for Pre-Nursing
sheets.overview: The Nurse Aide curriculum prepares students to work under the supervision of licensed healthcare professionals in performing nursing care and services for persons of all ages. Instruction focuses on growth and development throughout the life span, personal care, vital signs, communication, nutrition, medical asepsis, therapeutic activities, accident and fire safety, household environment and equipment management, as well as employment skills and family resources and services.
sheets.employmentOutlook: Program completers may qualify for Nursing Aide I registry listing and find employment in home health agencies, hospitals, clinics, nursing homes, extended care facilities, and doctors' offices.

sheets.title: Associate in Applied Science
sheets.overview: The Physical Therapist Assistant curriculum prepares graduates for direct patient care settings under physical therapists' supervision. Assistants work to enhance or restore function by addressing physical impairment. Coursework encompasses normal human anatomy and physiology, the consequences of disease or injury, and physical therapy treatment of various patient conditions affecting humans throughout their lifespan.
sheets.employmentOutlook: Upon graduation, students may take the PTA National Physical Therapy Exam (NPTE) for licensure. Employment opportunities exist in general hospitals, rehabilitation centers, outpatient orthopedic clinics, school systems, geriatric health care facilities, and home health agencies.

rows.name: Introduction to Communication or Public Speaking (COM 110 or COM 231)
Survey of Medical Terminology or Medical Terminology I (MED 120 or MED 121)

sheets.title: Associate in Applied Science
sheets.overview: The Occupational Therapy Assistant curriculum prepares individuals to operate under a licensed occupational therapist's direction in screening, assessing, planning, and implementing treatment as well as documenting progress for clients receiving occupational therapy services. Coursework emphasizes human growth and development, conditions that interfere with activities of daily living, theory and process of occupational therapy, individual or group treatment activities, the therapeutic use of self, activity analysis as well as grading or adapting activities and environments.
sheets.employmentOutlook: Graduates may qualify for the national certification examination for certified occupational therapy assistant practice. Employment settings include hospitals, rehabilitation centers, extended care facilities, sheltered workshops, schools, outpatient clinics, home health programs, and community-based services.

sheets.title: Associate in Applied Science
sheets.overview: The Surgical Technology curriculum prepares individuals to assist in surgical patient care within operating rooms and function as surgical team members. Students apply theoretical knowledge to patient care during surgery and develop competencies in preparing supplies, equipment and instruments; maintaining aseptic conditions; preparing patients for surgery; and assisting surgeons during operations. Graduates take the National Board of Surgical Technology and Surgical Assisting Certification Examination before graduation.
sheets.employmentOutlook: Employment settings include labor/delivery/emergency departments, inpatient/outpatient surgery centers, dialysis units, endoscopy suites, physician offices, and central supply processing units.

sheets.title: Associate in Applied Science
sheets.overview: The Respiratory Therapy curriculum prepares individuals to function as professional respiratory therapists, performing diagnostic tests, physical exams, treatments, patient and family education, assisting physicians with procedures, and managing patients with heart and lung diseases. Students develop competencies in treating cardiopulmonary conditions through assessment, life support, monitoring, and drug administration involving patients of all ages in various settings including emergency room, critical care, and general floor.
sheets.employmentOutlook: Upon completion, graduates qualify for the Therapist Multiple-Choice Examination and Clinical Simulation Examination administered by the National Board for Respiratory Care. Employment opportunities exist in acute and long-term care hospitals, clinics, physician offices, nursing homes, education, industry, and home care.

rows.name: Survey of Medical Terminology or Medical Terminology I (MED 120 or MED 121)
Basic Anatomy & Physiology or Anatomy and Physiology I & II (BIO 163 or BIO 168/169)
Mathematics elective (MAT 143 or MAT 152 or MAT 171)
Writing elective (ENG 112 or ENG 113 or ENG 114)

sheets.title: Associate in Applied Science
sheets.subtitle: Agnes Binder Weisiger Cardiovascular Technology
sheets.overview: The Cardiovascular Technology curriculum prepares individuals to develop attributes necessary to perform procedures leading to the diagnosis and treatment of cardiovascular disease. Coursework emphasizes technical and cognitive skills including echocardiography, exercise stress testing, electrocardiography, cardiac catheterization, blood gases, electrophysiology, and cardiovascular anatomy and physiology.
sheets.employmentOutlook: Graduates selecting the non-invasive track (Cardiac Sonography) qualify for the Registered Diagnostic Cardiac Sonographer (RDCS) credential through ARDMS or Registered Cardiac Sonography (RCS) through CCI. Invasive track graduates may pursue Registered Cardiovascular Invasive Specialist (RCIS) credentials through CCI. Graduates may work in hospitals, clinics, and cardiology offices.

rows.name: Basic Anatomy & Physiology or Anatomy and Physiology I (BIO 163 or BIO 168)
Survey of Medical Terminology or Medical Terminology I (MED 120 or MED 121)
Cardiac and Peripheral Vascular Invasive I
Conceptual Physics or College Physics I (PHY 110/110A or PHY 151)
Cardiac and Peripheral Vascular Invasive II
Cardiac and Peripheral Vascular Invasive III

sheets.title: Associate in Applied Science
sheets.overview: The Polysomnography curriculum equips learners with competencies to critically assess, plan, implement, and evaluate clinical care and management of sleep disorders and daytime alertness. Students work as part of a clinical team under physician supervision, applying specialized knowledge in evaluating and treating sleep conditions across all age groups. The curriculum covers diagnostic testing including polysomnograms, PAP titration studies, multiple sleep latency tests, maintenance of wakefulness tests, actigraphy, and home sleep testing.
sheets.employmentOutlook: Employment opportunities exist in facility-based or private sleep diagnostic and treatment centers, durable medical equipment companies, medical specialty sales, research, and more.

sheets.title: Associate in Applied Science
sheets.overview: The Medical Laboratory Technology curriculum trains individuals to execute clinical lab procedures across chemistry, hematology, microbiology, and transfusion medicine for health maintenance and disease diagnosis and treatment. Coursework emphasizes mathematical and scientific concepts related to specimen collection, laboratory testing and procedures, quality assurance in reporting/recording and interpreting findings involving tissues, blood, or body fluids.
sheets.employmentOutlook: Graduates qualify to sit for American Society of Clinical Pathology Board of Certification exams. Career opportunities span hospital labs, physician offices, blood banks, industry, and research settings.

sheets.title: Associate in Applied Science
sheets.overview: The Pharmacy Technology curriculum prepares individuals to assist the pharmacist in duties that a pharmacy technician can legally perform within prescribed boundaries. Students engage with healthcare and pharmaceutical principles, emphasizing the pharmacy technician as a member of the interdisciplinary team providing safe, individualized care. Work includes prescription preparation, intravenous solution mixing, patient profile management, inventory maintenance, medication packaging, and data gathering for drug therapy monitoring.
sheets.employmentOutlook: Graduates qualify to pursue Pharmacy Technician Certification Board (PTCB) certification. Employment opportunities span retail, hospitals, nursing homes, research laboratories, and pharmaceutical manufacturing facilities.

sheets.title: Associate in Applied Science
sheets.overview: The Dental Hygiene curriculum prepares individuals to assess, plan, implement, and evaluate dental hygiene care for the individual and the community. Students develop competencies in patient assessment, care planning, oral hygiene instruction, radiographic imaging, and preventive therapy. The program emphasizes electronic patient management systems and procedures within dental hygiene scope of practice.
sheets.employmentOutlook: Graduates qualify to pursue national and state licensure examinations required for practice. Career opportunities span dental offices, hospital clinics, schools, public health agencies, and educational institutions.

sheets.title: Diploma
sheets.overview: The Dental Assisting curriculum prepares individuals to assist the dentist in delivering dental treatment and function as integral members of the dental team while performing chairside and related office and laboratory procedures. Instruction encompasses general studies, biomedical sciences, dental sciences, clinical sciences, and clinical practice through lecture, laboratory, and clinical experiences. Students gain knowledge in infection/hazard control, radiography, dental materials, preventive dentistry, head and neck anatomy, dental and medical emergencies, and clinical procedures.
sheets.employmentOutlook: Graduates may qualify for the Dental Assisting National Board Examination to become Certified Dental Assistants and work as Dental Assistant II (per North Carolina Dental Laws) in dental offices and related settings.

sheets.title: Associate in Applied Science
sheets.overview: The Ophthalmic Medical Personnel program prepares individuals to perform ophthalmic procedures under a licensed physician specializing in Ophthalmology. Coursework encompasses lecture, laboratory, and clinical training covering ocular measurements, testing, lensometry, medication administration, eye care, and instrument maintenance.
sheets.employmentOutlook: Diploma graduates may qualify for Certified Ophthalmic Assistant certification; Associate degree graduates may pursue Certified Ophthalmic Technician certification through the Joint Commission on Allied Health Personnel in Ophthalmology National Certification Exam. Graduates work in medical institutions, clinics, or physician practices.

rows.name: Introduction to Diseases of the Eye
Introduction to Communication or Public Speaking (COM 110 or COM 231)
Writing and Research in the Disciplines

sheets.title: Associate in Applied Science
sheets.overview: The Emergency Medical Science curriculum prepares graduates for careers as entry-level paramedics. The curriculum encompasses basic and advanced life support knowledge and skills through classroom, laboratory, and hospital clinical instruction at a regional Level One trauma center. Field internship occurs at a major EMS provider. The core focus includes EMS management, rescue operations management, and EMS education methods, providing leadership experience.
sheets.employmentOutlook: Upon completion, students qualify for North Carolina and National Paramedic certification exams. Employment prospects include EMS agencies, fire and rescue, critical care transport, special events, and government positions.

rows.name: Professional Research & Reporting or Introduction to Communication (ENG 114 or COM 110)

sheets.title: Associate in Applied Science
sheets.overview: The Medical Assisting curriculum prepares multi-skilled health care professionals to be qualified to perform administrative, clinical, and laboratory procedures. Coursework encompasses scheduling appointments, coding and processing insurance accounts, billing, collections, medical transcription, computer operations, assisting with examinations and treatments, performing routine laboratory procedures, electrocardiography, supervised medication administration, and ethical/legal issues associated with patient care.
sheets.employmentOutlook: Graduates are prepared for employment in physician offices, clinics, hospitals, and other healthcare settings where multi-skilled clinical and administrative support is needed.

rows.name: Introduction to Communication or Public Speaking (COM 110 or COM 231)
Medical Insurance Coding or Diet Therapy or Nutrition or College Transfer Success or Medical Communication (MED 232/274/BIO 155/ACA 122/HCI 120)
